content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors in the program to get the desired output.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer choice.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output one line containing the corresponding message:
- "Option 1 selected" if choice == 1
- "Option 2 selected" if choice == 2
- "Option 3 selected" if choice == 3
- "Invalid choice" for any other value.

sampleTestCases.output:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Invalid choice
sampleTestCases.explanation: Three test cases are processed. Choices 1 and 2 map to their respective messages, while 5 is not a valid option.
Choice 3 yields "Option 3 selected". Choice 0 is outside the defined range, so "Invalid choice" is printed.
A single test case with choice 2 prints the corresponding message.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected
Option 3 selected
Invalid choice
Invalid choice

hidden.label: edge case - repeated valid choice
hidden.expectedOutput:
Option 3 selected
Option 3 selected
Option 3 selected

hidden.label: general case with negatives and zero
hidden.expectedOutput:
Invalid choice
Invalid choice
Option 1 selected
Option 2 selected

hidden.label: general case with large out‑of‑range values

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times, reading each choice. Use a simple conditional (if‑else or switch) to map the integer to the required output string. Print the string followed by a newline. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used besides the output buffer.
